expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names destructive_operations.md as the first reference (merge_methods.md acceptable as the second stop for the merge-specific classmethod-discard mechanics)
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'Consult destructive_operations.md first' and then 'Why merge_methods.md is the second read, not the first'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Mentions inspect-before-destroy as the operative pattern; bonus credit for naming the classmethod-discard merge gotcha
expectations.evidence: Five-phase workflow: Inspect → Report → Wait for explicit confirmation → Execute → Verify. Bonus: explicitly names the classmethod-discard footgun: '(PositionOutput & merge_key).merge_delete() silently drops the restriction because merge_delete is a classmethod with restriction=True as the default'.
